result:
All info gathered. Here's the complete build-gating chain for the e1000e driver:

**(a) Kconfig option enabling e1000e**
- `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58` — `config E1000E` (tristate, `depends on PCI && (!SPARC32 || BROKEN)`, `depends on PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL`, `select CRC32`)
- (Related, not required: `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:78` — `config E1000E_HWTS`, `depends on E1000E && X86`, default y — optional sub-feature, not needed to build the base driver.)

**(b) Makefile rule compiling e1000e's objects**
`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile`:
- Line 11: `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o`
- Lines 13–15: object list
  ```
  e1000e-objs := 82571.o ich8lan.o 80003es2lan.o \
                 mac.o manage.o nvm.o phy.o \
                 param.o ethtool.o netdev.o ptp.o
  ```

**(c) All build-system gating layers, from the driver up to the top**

1. **Driver-local Makefile** — `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11` — `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o` (the actual compile rule).
2. **Vendor-dir descend gate (Makefile)** — `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8` — `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/` (kbuild only descends into the `e1000e/` subdir when `CONFIG_E1000E` is set).
3. **Vendor-dir descend gate (Kconfig)** — `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58` sits inside an `if NET_VENDOR_INTEL` block, opened at `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:17` (`if NET_VENDOR_INTEL`) and closed at `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:359` (`endif # NET_VENDOR_INTEL`) — so `E1000E` is only selectable if `CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL` (defined `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:6`, `bool "Intel devices"`, `default y`) is enabled.
4. **"intel/" descend gate at ethernet level (Makefile)** — `drivers/net/ethernet/Makefile:50` — `obj-$(CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL) += intel/` (kbuild only descends into `intel/` when `CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL` is set).
5. **Kconfig source of the Intel submenu** — `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:86` — `source "drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig"`, itself under the `menuconfig ETHERNET` declared at `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:6`.
6. **"ethernet/" descend gate (Makefile)** — `drivers/net/Makefile:52` — `obj-$(CONFIG_ETHERNET) += ethernet/` (only descends into `ethernet/` when `CONFIG_ETHERNET` is set, which comes from the `menuconfig ETHERNET` at `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:6`).
7. **Kconfig source of the ethernet menu** — `drivers/net/Kconfig:490` — `source "drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig"`.
8. **"net/" descend (top level, unconditional)** — `drivers/Makefile:95` — `obj-y += net/` (always descends; not config-gated).

So the effective condition chain to actually get `e1000e.o` object files built is:
`CONFIG_ETHERNET` (menuconfig) → `CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL` (default y) → `CONFIG_E1000E` (tristate, needs `PCI`, `!SPARC32||BROKEN`, `PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL`) — each enforced twice, once as a Makefile `obj-$(CONFIG_X) += subdir/` descend-gate and once as the Kconfig dependency/if-block that makes `CONFIG_X` reachable/settable in the first place.
